skill.name: minimal-run-and-audit
skill.description: Runs a selected short reproduction command, captures execution evidence, and standardizes README-first AI repository audit outputs.
skill.summary: Capture and report evidence from selected AI reproduction commands.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against the cited files. One medium-risk issue is confirmed: scripts/run_command.py intentionally executes a caller-selected command in the target repository. The weak-crypto, Ruby backtick, and reconnaissance detections are false positives caused by prose, markdown backticks, or hardcoded status checks.

medium_findings.title: Caller-Supplied Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: scripts/run_command.py runs the --command argument in the target repository with subprocess.run after shlex splitting. The implementation avoids shell metacharacter expansion, but it still launches arbitrary commands chosen by the caller.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is supplied through the required --command argument and passed to subprocess.run. This is legitimate for reproduction work, but it is still untrusted code execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Git Status Subprocess
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for command injection: run_git invokes git with an argument list for repository status capture. The cited call does not use shell=True and is limited to internal git status checks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess target is git and the observed call sites provide fixed status-related arguments. No evidence found that this path accepts arbitrary shell text.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: the Ruby backtick detections are markdown formatting or literal command text in output strings. No Ruby execution context or shell backtick evaluation appears at the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited SKILL.md and reporting-policy.md lines are documentation, and the Python line only formats a log message. None of these lines run Ruby or evaluate shell backticks.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: the cited lines are descriptions, headings, or timeout status text. No cryptographic algorithm use or system reconnaissance command is present at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found natural language and status strings at the cited lines. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, host probing, environment probing, or similar behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Arbitrary Process Launch
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper starts the selected command as a subprocess in the target repository. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should treat it as untrusted repository execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess.run call receives the parsed --command value. The risk is bounded by shell=False, but arbitrary binaries can still be launched.

content.value_statement: AI repository reproduction work often needs concise evidence from a selected smoke, inference, or evaluation command. This skill runs that chosen command and organizes the result into standardized audit outputs.
actual_capabilities: Runs a selected short non-training command in a target repository.
Captures stdout, stderr, return code, timeout state, and launch errors.
Extracts simple numeric metrics from command output.
Compares git status before and after execution to identify changed paths.
Produces structured evidence for SUMMARY.md, COMMANDS.md, LOG.md, and status output.
limitations: Does not choose the reproduction target or research question.
Does not perform training runs or manage long-running jobs.
Does not install missing dependencies or prepare datasets by itself.
Runs caller-selected commands, so untrusted repositories require sandboxing.

use_cases.title: Validate a README Smoke Test
use_cases.description: Run the documented quick command and record whether it completed, failed, or timed out.

use_cases.title: Audit a Model Inference Command
use_cases.description: Capture command output, metrics, and changed files from a short inference check.

use_cases.title: Normalize Reproduction Evidence
use_cases.description: Convert execution results into consistent reports for review or handoff.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Smoke Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this selected smoke command in the repository and summarize the result in the standard reproduction output files.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Inference Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the documented inference command with a short timeout, capture stdout and stderr, and report observed metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Repository Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the selected evaluation command and compare git status before and after execution to identify changed files.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Review-Ready Bundle
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute the approved non-training command, classify the outcome, extract metrics, and prepare concise reproduction evidence for reviewer handoff.

output_examples.input: A selected README smoke command with a 60 second timeout.
output_examples.output: A concise report states whether the command succeeded, failed, or timed out, with the main blocker and relevant log excerpts.

output_examples.input: A short inference command that prints accuracy and loss values.
output_examples.output: The output highlights observed metrics, identifies the best available metric, and records any changed repository paths.

output_examples.input: A documented evaluation command that exits with a nonzero status.
output_examples.output: The result is marked partial, the exit code is explained, and the captured stderr is preserved for review.

best_practices: Run only short, documented, non-training commands.
Use a clean sandbox when executing untrusted repository code.
Record dependency, asset, and timeout assumptions before execution.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to start training or long background jobs.
Letting the skill choose the research target without prior context.
Treating a failed smoke run as proof that the full repository is invalid.

faq.question: Does this skill choose which command to run?
faq.answer: No. It expects a selected smoke, inference, evaluation, or sanity command.

faq.question: Can it run training jobs?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for short non-training verification commands.

faq.question: What evidence does it capture?
faq.answer: It captures process output, exit status, timeout state, simple metrics, and git status changes.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Only with sandboxing. The skill runs the command selected by the caller.

faq.question: Does it install dependencies?
faq.answer: No. Environment setup should happen before using this skill.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
